item.title: 4 gantry cranes arrive for Patenga Container Terminal
item.description: Four state-of-the-art quay gantry cranes for the Patenga Container Terminal have arrived in Chattogram....

item.title: Govt urged to reconsider 10pc tax on private universities
item.description: Expressing deep concern over the decision to retain the existing 10 per cent income tax on private universities in the proposed national budget for the financial year 2026–27, the Association of Private Universities of Bangladesh on Saturday urged......

item.title: Saudi expatriate found dead in Dhaka hotel, one held
item.description: A 40-year-old Bangladesh expatriate in Saudi Arabia was found dead at a hotel of Paltan in Dhaka on Friday....

item.title: Violence, abuse keep women from active politics: study
item.description: Violence against women in politics, both online and offline, remains a major barrier to their participation and leadership in Bangladesh, according to a study jointly done by the BRAC Institute of Governance and Development and UN Women....

item.title: Israeli strikes kill five in Gaza
item.description: Gaza health officials said Israeli strikes on Saturday killed five people, including four members of the same family, in the latest violence to rock the Palestinian territory despite a ceasefire....

item.title: Russian strike kills one, wounds nine in Kharkiv
item.description: A Russian aerial attack killed one person and wounded nine others in Kharkiv city in eastern Ukraine, local authorities said on Saturday....

item.title: NCP serves notices on two Ctg leaders over disciplinary breach
item.description: The National Citizen Party on Saturday served show-cause notices on its Chattogram city unit convener Mir Mohammad Sohaib and member secretary Arif Moinuddin for publicly taking a position over a sexual harassment allegation against a......

item.title: Prisoner dies in custody
item.description: A 68-year-old prisoner died at Dhaka Medical College Hospital on Saturday after reportedly suffering cardiac arrest in Dhaka Central Jail....

item.title: PM calls for united efforts to harness maritime potential
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman on Saturday called for harnessing the country’s maritime potential through coordinated, organised and united efforts by all stakeholders, while giving the highest priority to national interests....

item.title: FM urges UK to sustain leadership role in Rohingya repatriation
item.description: Foreign minister Khalilur Rahman on Saturday urged the United Kingdom to maintain its leadership role in international efforts to ensure the safe, voluntary, dignified and sustainable repatriation of forcibly displaced Rohingyas to Myanmar....

item.title: BNP restored multi-party democracy: Fakhrul
item.description: Secretary-general of the Bangladesh Nationalist Party and local government, rural development and cooperatives minister Mirza Fakhrul Islam Alamgir on Saturday said that the BNP had restored multi-party democracy and did not believe in one-party system of governance....

item.title: Gazipur water sickens factory workers again
item.description: Around 50 workers at a garment factory in Gazipur fell ill Saturday morning, just two days after more than 100 workers reportedly suffered similar illnesses at the same factory....

item.title: 2 held with 31 gold bars in Jashore
item.description: Members of the Border Guard Bangladesh arrested two people and seized 31 gold bars weighing 3.641kg from a car on the Jashore-Narail Highway in Jashore early Saturday....

item.title: 61,697 Hajj pilgrims return home
item.description: A total of 61,697 Hajj pilgrims have returned home from Saudi Arabia as of Friday, following the completion of this year’s Hajj held on May 26....

item.title: Dhaka-Ctg train services disrupted for 2.5 hours
item.description: Train services on Dhaka-Chattigram route were disrupted for around two and a half hours as a mechanical fault developed in the engine of Meghna Express at Feni Railway Station on Saturday morning, leaving at least 800 passengers stranded....

item.title: NATIONAL TEA DAY: Fair wages, housing for workers demanded
item.description: Discussants at a programme in Moulvibazar on Saturday said that the government must pay greater attention to the welfare of tea garden workers and their families to ensure the sustainable development of tea industry....

item.title: 3rd edition of Turaag Active Dhaka concludes at Hatirjheel
item.description: The third edition of Turaag Active Dhaka 25K 2026 concluded at Hatirjheel in Dhaka on Friday, bringing together nearly 3,000 professional and amateur runners from Bangladesh and abroad in one of the country’s largest road-running events....

item.title: Experts skeptical about budget implementation
item.description: Speakers in a webinar on Saturday lauded different proposals of the national budget but expressed doubt about its overall success in absence of its implementation strategies......

item.title: Govt gives priority to skill-based, job-oriented education system: UGC chair
item.description: Chairman of the University Grants Commission of Bangladesh Professor Mamun Ahmed at a conference in Dhaka on Saturday said that the government and the UGC were giving utmost importance to skill-based and job-oriented education system....

item.title: Textile millers seek retention of 30pc value addition rule
item.description: The country’s textile millers on Saturday urged the government to retain the existing 30 per cent value-addition condition for raw material imports, backed by bank guarantees, primarily by non-bonded manufacturers, in the proposed national budget for the financial year 2026–27......

item.title: Activists allege cultural budget meagre, slam govt
item.description: Cultural activists, including filmmakers and theatre people, castigated the government on Saturday, alleging that the allocations for cultural activities in the proposed national budget covered nothing more than the ministry’s operational costs....

item.title: Foundation stone of DIIT permanent campus laid at Manda
item.description: Daffodil Institute of IT laid the foundation stone of its permanent campus at the Green Model Town of Manda in Dhaka on Saturday....

item.title: BGMEA holds 43rd AGM
item.description: The 43rd annual general meeting of the Bangladesh Garment Manufacturers and Exporters Association was held on Saturday at the BGMEA Complex in the capital, according to a press release......

item.title: Joyanta’s ‘Branches of Solace’ screened in Malaysian festival
item.description: Filmmaker Joyanta Gayen’s short film titled Branches of Solace was screened at the 22nd international Mini Film Festival in Malaysia on Friday....

item.title: Digital Summit focuses on AI, storytelling in marketing
item.description: The 12th Digital Summit brought together leading marketers, brand professionals, agency executives and technology experts in Dhaka on Saturday to discuss how artificial intelligence, data analytics and evolving consumer behaviour are reshaping the future of digital transformation......

item.title: Poet Al Mujahidi laid to rest
item.description: Ekushey Padak-winning poet, freedom fighter, and long-time literary editor of The Daily Ittefaq Al Mujahidi laid to rest at the Martyred Intellectuals’ Graveyard in Mirpur on Saturday....

item.title: PM assures of full support to reopen closed factories
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman on Saturday assured investors of full government support in reviving non-operational and loss-making state-owned factories, saying the government is committed to removing any obstacles and creating a more business-friendly environment......

item.title: BAAMA seeks EV-equivalent incentives for PHEVs
item.description: Bangladesh Automobiles Assemblers and Manufacturers Association has urged the government to provide locally assembled and manufactured plug-in hybrid electric vehicles with the same tariff benefits and incentives as electric vehicles, warning that the proposed budget may discourage domestic investment in the automobile sector......

item.title: CCCI team meets Chinese company Alibaba
item.description: A courtesy call and a discussion took place with the leaders of the Chattogram Chamber of Commerce and Industry (CCCI) and a delegation from the China-based e-commerce firm Alibaba.com at the World Trade Center in the city on Saturday......

item.title: Gold price drops by Tk 6,590 per bhari in two days
item.description: Bangladesh Jewellers Association on Saturday further reduced gold prices by Tk 2,216 per bhari, bringing the price of 22-carat gold down to Tk 226,340 per bhari amid a decline in the domestic price of pure gold......

item.title: Takaful Islami Insurance AGM approves 10 pc cash dividend
item.description: Takaful Islami Insurance PLC organised its 26th annual general meeting on Saturday at 11:00am virtually, where a 10 per cent cash dividend was approved......

item.title: Ship traffic thru Hormuz climbs after war deal
item.description: Shipping traffic through the Strait of Hormuz rose to its busiest in two months after a deal to halt the US-Iran war, maritime trackers said on Friday......

item.title: Tech-based training for young professionals held in Rajshahi
item.description: A daylong seminar for the salesmen of the app-based company Otithi.com was held in Rajshahi......

item.title: A decade on, Brexit still bites for UK small businesses
item.description: Ten years on from a referendum that triggered Britain’s exit from the European Union, the nation’s small- and medium-size businesses find themselves caught between mountains of paperwork, rising costs and dashed expectations......

item.title: Europe eyes banking sans frontières
item.description: Creating large banks which can operate across Europe is desirable for sustaining the continent’s financial system, the European Central Bank’s chief economist said Friday......

item.title: Rangpur groundnut prices hit record high
item.description: Farmers are very happy in Rangpur agricultural region after getting a bumper production of groundnut during the last 2025-2026 Rabi season and its all-time high prices in local markets......

item.title: Food grain stocks in country exceed 20.6 lakh tonnes
item.description: The government food grain reserves remain at a satisfactory level, helping ensure the country’s food security......

item.title: Top-listed crime suspect Palash dies from bullet wounds
item.description: Listed crime suspect Yeasin Khan, widely known as Kaillya Palash, who was injured in a shooting in the capital’s Rampura on June 12, died at a city hospital early Saturday....

item.title: Iraq oil production to return to pre-war levels in two months
item.description: Iraqi authorities predict oil production will return to peacetime levels ‘within one to two months’, state media reported, after the Middle East war caused exports to plummet......

item.title: Political climate continues to constrain media freedom
item.description: THE arrest of journalists is a worrying reminder of the authoritarian legacy of the fallen Awami League regime and a departure from the Bangladesh Nationalist Party’s electoral promises of media freedom. The acting editor of the daily Agrajatra Pratidin was arrested and sent to jail on June 19 on allegations of reporting defamatory news about the state minister for local......

item.title: Gap in reproductive health care is gap in justice
item.description: MENSTRUAL health among adolescent girls in rural areas remains a widespread under-addressed public health concern, with new evidence showing that it significantly disrupts education, well-being and access to information. An International Centre for Diarrhoeal Diseases Research, Bangladesh study, conducted in 2023–2025, has found that nearly two in three girls......

item.title: Law, humanity stranded in no man’s land
item.description: CAUGHT between two states and claimed by neither, they wait under the open sky. Some have spent days in the narrow strips of land that separate Bangladesh and India. Others have been held for weeks in detention centres in India before being driven to the border in darkness. Among them are women clutching frightened children, elderly people exhausted by......

item.title: Why arts matter more than ever
item.description: AFTER the news that the government was considering removing bachelor courses in literature, history and philosophy had been debunked by government officials, some influential voices on social media have advanced a more ‘moderate’ idea: downsizing the courses into minors embedded within other, supposedly ‘more important’ degrees. They cite examples from......

item.title: Dynastic politics and inclusive development crisis
item.description: The 2024 uprising represented a historic rejection of authoritarianism, political arrogance, personality cults and the culture of state capture by a privileged few. Millions of Bangladeshis envisioned a country where public resources would be distributed based on citizens’ needs rather than political identity, family connections or geographical loyalty. Yet, the emerging......

item.title: Building a new Asia
item.description: I HAD the great honour of speaking in the Gedung Merdeka (Independence Hall) in Bandung, Indonesia in April. I was struck not by nostalgia, but by urgency. Bandung is not a museum piece, but a living political inheritance......

item.title: France's Saliba playing through the pain at World Cup
item.description: France centre-half William Saliba said Saturday he has been ‘gritting his teeth’ and playing through the pain at the World Cup after suffering from a back problem for the past few months....

item.title: Marmoush aims to emerge from Salah's shadow
item.description: Omar Marmoush once rejected the chance to play for Canada, but in Vancouver on Sunday will aim to become a national hero back home in Egypt by making World Cup history. ...

item.title: 'Fired up' Spain ready to hit back: De la Fuente
item.description:
Spain boss Luis de la Fuente said his players have been stung by the criticism that followed a shock 0-0 draw against Cape Verde, but will put that right against Saudi Arabia on Sunday.
...

item.title: Raphinha to have 'intensive treatment' on thigh injury
item.description:
Brazil forward Raphinha will undergo ‘intensive treatment’ after suffering a muscle injury to his right thigh in their World Cup win over Haiti, the Brazilian football confederation said Saturday.
...

item.title: Ronaldinho coming out of retirement to join Italian 3rd division side
item.description:
Former Ballon d'Or and World Cup winner Ronaldinho is set to come out of retirement aged 46 to play for Ravenna in the Italian third tier, according to reports in Italian daily Gazzetta dello Sport.
...

item.title: Germany come from behind to reach last 32
item.description: Germany reached the World Cup knockout phase for the first time since 2014 with a dramatic come-from-behind 2-1 victory over Ivory Coast on Saturday. ...

item.title: Israeli army kills journalist in Gaza
item.description: The Israeli military said on Saturday it had carried out a strike that killed Al Jazeera journalist Ahmed Wishah in Gaza, claiming that he was a .Hamas terrorist’......

item.title: Netherlands thump Sweden in Houston to get World Cup liftoff
item.description: Cody Gakpo and Brian Brobbey both scored twice as the rampant Netherlands thrashed Sweden 5-1 in a World Cup warning on Saturday in Houston....
